name: Self Reliance NY Federal Credit Union
description_short: Federal credit union established in 1951 serving Ukrainian Americans with $1B+ in assets, offering checking, savings, IRAs, mortgages, and online banking across five NY-area branches.
description_long:
Self Reliance NY Federal Credit Union was founded in 1951 by volunteers under the sponsorship of the New York Branch of the Selfreliance Association of American Ukrainians. It operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution dedicated to the philosophy of "people helping people," and holds the distinction of being the oldest and largest Ukrainian credit union in the world. The organization serves over 15,000 members with assets exceeding $1 billion.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), real estate lending with mortgages featuring low rates and flexible terms, VISA EZCards, online and mobile banking available 24/7, and ATM access. Members can access services through five physical branch locations in New York: the headquarters on Second Avenue in Manhattan, plus branches in Astoria, Kerhonkson, Lindenhurst, and Uniondale. The institution emphasizes personalized service with stated commitments to confidentiality, professionalism, and member value.

A significant recent development is the October 1, 2025 merger with Ukrainian Selfreliance Federal Credit Union, resulting in the rebranded "Self Reliance Financial Federal Credit Union." The organization is currently integrating products, services, systems, and procedures across all physical and digital channels. This merger represents a substantial consolidation within the Ukrainian-American credit union space and may affect member accounts during the transition period.

As a federally-chartered credit union, Self Reliance NY operates under regulatory oversight and membership restrictions tied to the Ukrainian-American community connection. While the institution boasts strong capitalization and longevity, potential members should note the ongoing system integration from the recent merger and limited branch hours, particularly at smaller locations like Kerhonkson which operates only three days per week with restricted hours.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Over $1 billion in assets provides institutional stability and member protection
Longest-established Ukrainian credit union globally with 73+ years of operating history
24/7 online and mobile banking availability for account access and transactions
Five physical branch locations across New York (Manhattan, Queens, Long Island, upstate)
Mortgage lending with explicitly advertised low rates and flexible terms
Extended Saturday hours (8:30 AM - 2:00 PM) at multiple branches for working members
Member-owned cooperative structure aligned with non-profit philosophy
cons: Limited branch hours at smaller locations (Kerhonkson open only Tue/Thu/Sat 10 AM-1 PM)
Ongoing system integration from October 2025 merger may cause service disruptions or temporary access issues
Membership restricted to Ukrainian-American community members or association affiliations
No mention of checking account overdraft protections, fee structures, or interest rates on savings products
Geographically concentrated service area limited to New York state only
best_for: Ukrainian-American families seeking community-rooted banking with shared cultural values
Long-term members wanting to maintain relationships through a stable, well-capitalized institution
New York-area residents needing mortgage lending with personalized service
Members prioritizing member-owned cooperative structure over for-profit banking
diagnosis: Self Reliance NY FCU is best for Ukrainian-American community members in New York seeking stable, member-focused banking with a 73-year track record and $1B+ in assets. The primary caveat is the ongoing October 2025 merger integration, which may affect system stability and service delivery, plus highly limited hours at smaller branches and membership eligibility restrictions to the Ukrainian-American community.
services: Online banking with enrollment and login
Member disclosure and account integration support
Financial goal planning and advisory services
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
